title: Fix Team Invite Bug with Base URL Lookup
story: As a DataTools Pro user inviting a colleague, I want the invite email to work correctly so that my colleague can accept and join my account.
description:
## Bug
Team invite emails sent from the staging server were failing when the invited user tried to accept. The same-org validation check was broken.

## Root Cause
The system checks whether both users belong to the same Salesforce org by comparing their `base_url` (login.salesforce.com for production, test.salesforce.com for sandbox). The query was returning a multi-row array instead of a single row for the user, causing the comparison to fail.

## Fix
Updated the base_url lookup to return only a single row per user instead of the full array.
acceptance_criteria: Team invite acceptance works when both users are from the same Salesforce org
Base URL lookup returns a single row per user
Production vs. sandbox org differentiation works correctly
middleware_changes: Fixed base_url lookup to return a single user row instead of a multi-row array, correcting same-org validation for team invites.
implementation_notes: The base_url field distinguishes production (login.salesforce.com) from sandbox (test.salesforce.com) orgs. The query was not using LIMIT 1 or equivalent, returning multiple rows and breaking the comparison logic.
comment_summary: Developer identified the multi-row array issue in the base_url query and fixed it to return a single row. Reporter confirmed the fix.
